findings.description: The entire Typography Hierarchy table (font family per level/channel: Agenda/Nunito Sans/Aptos, weight availability lists) has no corresponding rule in the candidate set. This is a large, distinctly governed content block (font assignment by level and channel, with an explicit digital exception for Agenda-as-live-text) that is missing entirely rather than being under- or over-clustered.

payload.description: Font family assignment per hierarchy level and channel (Agenda/Nunito Sans/Aptos), including digital exception allowing Agenda as live text for campaign headline only, and per-font weight availability.

findings.description: The [GENERAL]-tagged email font stack/fallback rule (CSS stack, @font-face loading requirement, Outlook fallback rationale) is not represented by any candidate rule and its cross-brand [GENERAL] governance tag is not captured anywhere in the group.

payload.description: Email font-family fallback stack requirement with webfont loading and Outlook fallback guarantee.

findings.description: Unit u0019 contains two distinct governance-tagged obligations bundled into one rule: a brand-specific accessibility minimum (150% line-height, no GENERAL tag) and a [GENERAL] conditional escalation (160% for light-on-dark). Merging them obscures the differing governance scope (brand-specific vs cross-brand GENERAL) and the conditional trigger.

rules.description: Minimum 150% line-height for all body copy (brand accessibility rule).
Increase line-height to 160% (e.g., 16px/26px) for light text on dark backgrounds.

findings.description: The email type scale unit is explicitly tagged [GENERAL], indicating cross-brand applicability, but this governance facet is not carried onto the clustered rule. Additionally u0018 explicitly carves out ISI sizing as owned by a locked component (an exclusion/scope facet) that is missing from the rule.

exclusions: ISI sizing owned by locked component

findings.description: The cluster is uniformly tagged polarity=must, but it embeds explicit prohibitions: secondary/supporting body text 'never for primary content' (u0016) and 'never set running text below 12px' (u0018). These are must_not sub-constraints that are flattened into a single must polarity, losing the negative-scope information.

sub_constraints.description: Secondary/supporting body text (14px) must never be used for primary content.
Running text must never be set below 12px.

findings.description: The visual hierarchy usage rule (use a clear type scale so reading order is evident; never use two adjacent text blocks of same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels) is not represented by any candidate rule and is distinct from the numeric email type-scale specification.

payload.description: Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels; maintain a clear type scale/visual hierarchy.
